exercises.name: C Major Scale
exercises.tips: Play ascending, then reverse for descending. Three notes per string. Keep fingers curved and relaxed. This pattern transposable to any key.

exercises.name: C Major Triad Arpeggio
exercises.tips: Ascending root-position arpeggio: root, major 3rd, perfect 5th. Keep each note ringing as long as possible. Focus on clean string transitions.

exercises.name: C Major Pentatonic Scale
exercises.tips: Two notes per string across the full neck. The major pentatonic is the foundation of country, blues, and rock phrasing. Know it in all keys.

exercises.name: C Minor Triad Arpeggio
exercises.tips: Ascending root-position minor arpeggio: root, minor 3rd, perfect 5th. Let each note sustain. Be precise — arpeggios reveal sloppy technique immediately.

exercises.name: C Minor Pentatonic Scale
exercises.tips: Box 1 shape, two notes per string. The most essential scale in rock, blues, and metal. Internalise this in every key until it is completely automatic.

exercises.name: C Major Scale in Broken Thirds
exercises.tips: Play each note of the major scale followed by the note a 3rd higher. Ascending pairs across the neck. Develops melodic ear training and finger independence.

exercises.tips: Play each note of the major scale followed by the note a 3rd higher. Ascending pairs across the neck. Develops melodic ear training and finger independence.

exercises.name: C Major Scale in Broken Tenths
exercises.tips: Each note paired with a 10th (octave + 3rd) above. The widest interval in this series. Treat each pair as a two-note chord shape moving up the neck.

exercises.name: C Diatonic Seventh Arpeggios
exercises.tips: Major 7th arpeggio (Imaj7): root, major 3rd, perfect 5th, major 7th. The harmonic backbone of jazz and neo-soul. Know where every chord tone lives.
